shelfSag.title: Sag — Shelf 1
shelfSag.value: predicted long-term sag 13.85 in over a 93 1/16 in span
shelfSag.explain: Eastern White Pine at 3/4 in thick under the “Books” preset (40 lb/ft). Stiffness comes from MOE (8.5 GPa) and thickness cubed. Sustained load: includes ×2 creep (Wood Handbook ch. 4).

prov.rule: sag = Σ load cases (5wL⁴/384EI and friends, sustained cases ×2 creep) with E = 8.5 GPa, I = bh³/12 = 160,043 mm⁴, L = 2364 mm

predicted sag 0.02 in across the 18 3/8 in between aprons

strength.title: Strength — Top
strength.explain: Comfortably below the breaking stress of Eastern White Pine with the standard ×4 wood safety factor.

sagTop.title: Sag — Top between aprons
sagTop.value: predicted sag 0.02 in across the 18 3/8 in between aprons
sagTop.explain: Eastern White Pine at 3/4 in thick under the “Heavy storage” preset (75 lb/ft), checked as a 13 1/8 in-wide strip spanning between the aprons.

prov.rule: plate strip: span 466 mm between apron faces, effective width min(top, span/2 + 100) = 333 mm, I = 190,337 mm⁴

joints.title: Joint adequacy
joints.value: weakest: pocket screws at apron–leg — 41 lb per joint vs 110 lb capacity
joints.explain: Every joint carries its share of the load path with room to spare.
